title: A Selection From Alice in Wonderland

q.en: Whom does the White Rabbit mistake Alice for?
q.zh: 白兔把爱丽丝误认成了谁？

explain.en: The Rabbit calls: 'Why, Mary Ann, what are you doing here?' Alice says to herself, 'He took me for his maid.'
explain.zh: 兔子喊道：“哎呀，玛丽·安，你在这儿干什么？”爱丽丝心想：“他把我当成了他的女仆。”

q.en: When Alice grew too tall, she had to 'stoop to save her neck.' What does 'stoop' mean here?
q.zh: 爱丽丝变得太高时，她不得不“stoop（弯下身）”以免脖子折断。“stoop”在这里是什么意思？

explain.en: The text says 'She had to stoop to save her neck from being broken,' meaning she bent down because her head hit the ceiling.
explain.zh: 文中说“她不得不弯下身子以免脖子折断”，意思是她低下身，因为头撞到了天花板。

q.en: After drinking the unlabeled bottle, what happens to Alice?
q.zh: 喝下那个没有标签的瓶子后，爱丽丝发生了什么？

explain.en: The book says: 'It did indeed... she found her head against the ceiling' and she kept 'growing and growing.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“确实如此……她发现头已顶到天花板”，并且她“不停地长大”。

q.en: Why did Alice get stuck inside the little house?
q.zh: 爱丽丝为什么被卡在小房子里出不去？

explain.en: The book says 'there seemed to be no chance of ever getting out of the room again' because she had grown so large she filled it.
explain.zh: 书中说“似乎再也没机会走出房间了”，因为她长得太大，填满了整个房间。

q.en: The little pebbles thrown in through the window turned into what?
q.zh: 从窗口扔进来的小石子变成了什么？

explain.en: The book says: 'Alice noticed... the pebbles were all turning into little cakes as they lay on the floor.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“爱丽丝注意到……落在地上的石子全都变成了小蛋糕。”

q.en: Whom does the Rabbit send down the chimney to get Alice out?
q.zh: 兔子派谁从烟囱下去对付爱丽丝？

explain.en: The Rabbit says: 'Bill's to go down the chimney!' and Alice later kicks 'Bill' out with a sharp kick.
explain.zh: 兔子说：“让比尔从烟囱下去！”后来爱丽丝一脚把“比尔”踢了出去。

q.en: When the animals plan to burn the house down, Alice threatens them with her cat ____.
q.zh: 当动物们打算烧掉房子时，爱丽丝用她的猫什么来威胁它们？

explain.en: The book says: 'Alice called out as loud as she could, "If you do, I'll set Dinah at you!"'
explain.zh: 书中说：“爱丽丝用最大的声音喊道：‘你们敢烧，我就放黛娜咬你们！’”

q.en: Why does the author show Alice talking to herself throughout the story?
q.zh: 作者为什么让爱丽丝在故事中一直自言自语？

options.en: To reveal her thoughts and feelings
options.zh: 为了展现她的想法和感受

explain.en: Alice's many spoken thoughts ('He took me for his maid', 'I can do no more') let the reader hear her inner feelings and reactions.
explain.zh: 爱丽丝大量自言自语（“他把我当成了女仆”“我无能为力了”）让读者听到她内心的想法和反应。

q.en: After eating one of the little cakes, what happened to Alice?
q.zh: 吃了一块小蛋糕后，爱丽丝发生了什么？

options.en: She shrank small enough to escape

explain.en: The book says: 'She was delighted to find that she began shrinking. As soon as she was small enough to get through the door, she ran out.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“她高兴地发现自己开始缩小。一缩小到能穿过门，她就跑了出去。”

q.en: At the end of the selection, where does Alice escape to?
q.zh: 节选的结尾，爱丽丝逃到了哪里？

explain.en: The book says: 'she ran off as hard as she could, and soon found herself safe in a thick wood.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“她拼命跑开，不久就安全躲进了一片茂密的树林。”

prompt.en: Imagine a new silly situation Alice could face after escaping into the wood, and write a short paragraph describing it in the style of the book, including something that changes her size.
prompt.zh: 想象爱丽丝逃进树林后可能遇到的又一个荒诞情境，用本书的风格写一小段描述，并包含一样能改变她身高的东西。

en: Use a 'DRINK ME' or 'EAT ME' style item that changes her size, as in the book.
Show Alice's reaction by letting her talk to herself.
zh: 像书中一样，用一个“喝我”或“吃我”式的物品来改变她的体型。
让爱丽丝通过自言自语来表现她的反应。
